summary: Ferocious Jerry, on‑fire Phil, and a nonstop Other One/Dark Star marathon
blurb: The show roars from the opening Cryptical Envelopment into an unbroken surge of The Other One, New Potato Caboose and Cosmic Charlie. Highlights include a legendary Other One → Dark Star run, a blazing The Eleven, and a rapturous Cosmic Charlie, all captured with crystal‑clear sound. The encore Hey Jude is a rare Pigpen moment but falls short of the show’s overall fire.
review: On March 1, 1969 at the Fillmore West the Dead delivered one of their most ferocious early‑‘70s performances. Jerry Garcia’s tone was described as “perfect” and his propulsion drove the band through a seamless set that never let up – from the hypnotic opening pair of Cryptical Envelopments into The Other One, then ripping through New Potato Caboose and a high‑octane Cosmic Charlie. Phil Lesh matched him note‑for‑note, earning praise as “on fire” and delivering perhaps his best ever bass work on The Eleven and a soaring solo in the New Potato suite. Bob Weir added extra heat on The Eleven, while Tom Constanten (noted by reviewers as Brent) supplied bright organ textures, especially during the rare Hey Jude encore. The second set peaked with an epic Other One → Dark Star marathon that reviewers called “legendary,” followed by a spirited Saint Stephen and a sweet Turn On Your Lovelight finale. Across all six soundboard sources the audio is pristine – the 16‑track Kaplan multitrack being the clearest, but even the more modest transfers capture the band’s fire. This show stands out as a high‑energy, tightly locked‑in performance that epitomizes the Fillmore West era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are overwhelmingly positive, with four labeling the show as 'excellent', 'legendary' or 'superb'. Multiple sources highlight the same standout moments – the Other One/Dark Star run, Cosmic Charlie rager and The Eleven – indicating strong consensus. No recording reports consistent performance flaws; mixed‑sentiment entries still praise key songs. This results in a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent 16‑track soundboard capture with crystal‑clear audio that showcases Jerry’s ferocious tone and the nonstop flow of the set.

key_highlights: Ferocious Jerry and on‑fire Phil drive a nonstop high‑energy journey.
Legendary Other One → Dark Star marathon with seamless segues.
Cosmic Charlie rager stands out as a fresh, face‑melting performance.
Dupree's Diamond Blues acoustic showcase adds contrast and depth.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers a ferocious, perfect‑tone performance with soaring fret‑board explorations on The Other One, Dark Star and the acoustic Dupree’s.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh is ‘on fire’, offering his best‑ever bass work on The Eleven and a standout solo in New Potato Caboose, matching Jerry note‑for‑note.
band_performance.Bob: Weir adds blazing energy on The Eleven and fuels the set’s momentum with aggressive rhythm work.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard contributions (Tom Constanten) are highlighted as prime organ work during Dark Star and provide a rare bright touch on the Hey Jude encore.
